heading: Pollinator-friendly practices on roadsides and highway rights-of-way
label: 332 Pollinator-friendly practices on roadsides and highway rights-of-way
notes: Effective DateSection effective , see , set out as an Effective Date of 2021 Amendment note under .Oct. 1, 2021section 10003 of Pub. L. 117–58section 101 of this title
amendments: Effective DateSection effective , see , set out as an Effective Date of 2021 Amendment note under .Oct. 1, 2021section 10003 of Pub. L. 117–58section 101 of this title

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a program to provide grants to eligible entities to carry out activities to benefit pollinators on roadsides and highway rights-of-way, including the planting and seeding of native, locally-appropriate grasses and wildflowers, including milkweed.
a State department of transportation;
an Indian tribe; or
a Federal land management agency.

children.heading: .—Application
children.label: c .—Application
children.text: To be eligible to receive a grant under this section, an eligible entity shall submit to the Secretary an application at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Secretary may require, including a pollinator-friendly practices plan described in subsection (d).

children.label: i
children.text: reducing the mowing swath outside of the State-designated safety zone;

children.label: ii
children.text: increasing the mowing height;

children.label: iii
children.text: reducing the mowing frequency;

children.label: iv
children.text: refraining from mowing monarch and other pollinator habitat during periods in which monarchs or other pollinators are present;

children.label: v
children.text: use of a flushing bar and cutting at reduced speeds to reduce pollinator deaths due to mowing; or

children.label: vi
children.text: reducing raking along roadsides and highway rights-of-way;

children.label: B
children.text: implementation of an integrated vegetation management plan that includes approaches such as mechanical tree and brush removal, targeted and judicious use of herbicides, and mowing, to address weed issues on roadsides and highway rights-of-way;

children.label: C
children.text: planting or seeding of native, locally-appropriate grasses and wildflowers, including milkweed, on roadsides and highway rights-of-way to enhance pollinator habitat, including larval host plants;

children.label: D
children.text: removing nonnative grasses from planting and seeding mixes, except for use as nurse or cover crops;

children.label: i
children.text: native plant identification;

children.label: ii
children.text: establishment and management of locally-appropriate native plants that benefit pollinators;

children.label: iii
children.text: land management practices that benefit pollinators; and

children.label: iv
children.text: pollinator-focused integrated vegetation management; or

children.label: F
children.text: any other pollinator-friendly practices the Secretary determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: .—Coordination
children.label: 2 .—Coordination
children.text: In developing a plan under paragraph (1), an eligible entity that is a State department of transportation or a Federal land management agency shall coordinate with applicable State agencies, including State agencies with jurisdiction over agriculture and fish and wildlife.

children.label: A
children.text: an eligible entity that is a State department of transportation or a Federal land management agency shall consult with affected or interested Indian tribes; and

children.label: B
children.text: any eligible entity may consult with nonprofit organizations, institutions of higher education, metropolitan planning organizations, and any other relevant entities.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretary shall provide a grant to each eligible entity that submits an application under subsection (c), including a plan under subsection (d), that the Secretary determines to be satisfactory.

children.label: A
children.text: shall be based on the number of pollinator-friendly practices the eligible entity has implemented or plans to implement; and

children.label: B
children.text: shall not exceed $150,000.

children.heading: .—Use of Funds
children.label: f .—Use of Funds
children.text: An eligible entity that receives a grant under this section shall use the funds for the implementation, improvement, or further development of the plan under subsection (d).

children.heading: .—Federal Share
children.label: g .—Federal Share
children.text: The Federal share of the cost of an activity carried out with a grant under this section shall be 100 percent.

children.heading: .—Best Practices
children.label: h .—Best Practices
children.text: The Secretary shall develop and make available to eligible entities best practices for, and a priority ranking of, pollinator-friendly practices on roadsides and highway rights-of-way.

children.heading: .—Technical Assistance
children.label: i .—Technical Assistance
children.text: On request of an eligible entity that receives a grant under this section, the Secretary shall provide technical assistance with the implementation, improvement, or further development of a plan under subsection (d).

children.heading: .—Administrative Costs
children.label: j .—Administrative Costs
children.text: For each fiscal year, the Secretary may use not more than 2 percent of the amounts made available to carry out this section for the administrative costs of carrying out this section.

children.heading: .—Report
children.label: k .—Report
children.text: Not later than 1 year after the date on which the first grant is provided under this section, the Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Environment and Public Works of the Senate and the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives a report on the implementation of the program under this section.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $2,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2022 through 2026.

children.heading: .—Availability
children.label: 2 .—Availability
children.text: Amounts made available under this section shall remain available for a period of 3 years after the last day of the fiscal year for which the funds are authorized.
